title: bursera jorullensis
intro: Bursera jorullensis, also known as Mexican copal, is a type of deciduous tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The resin of this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Bursera jorullensis include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting skin health. Bursera jorullensis can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, ointments, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
